entry.title: Washington Post: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: European Commission backs Kyiv’s E.U. ambitions; Putin claims the West wants to ‘cancel’ Russia
  • European Commission recommends E.U. candidate status for Ukraine
  • A Catholic bishop, under fire for abuse record, declines cardinal honor
  • China launches cutting-edge aircraft carrier, first to be locally designed
  • Top British official orders Julian Assange’s extradition to U.S.
  • Mexico City trades colorful food stalls for drab uniformity
  • U.N. turns to crowdfunding to salvage decaying oil tanker off Yemen
  • As nuclear test looms, North Korea reports intestinal epidemic
  • Third American missing in Ukraine identified as Marine veteran
  • Colombia’s historic presidential election: What you need to know
  • The troubled paradox of U.S. democracy
  • Ukraine war pushes global displaced to record high, U.N. says
  • Alleged Russian spy studied at Johns Hopkins, won ICC internship
  • In Kyiv visit, European leaders back Ukraine’s candidacy to join E.U.
  • France, Germany, Italy back Ukraine’s E.U. candidacy in first wartime trip to Kyiv
  • Myanmar junta vows to execute pro-democracy activists
  • Burkina Faso massacre highlights a strengthening insurgency
  • Estonian leader urges faster help for Ukraine amid signs of war fatigue
  • Canada: Mounties arrest priest for assault at school
  • Tanzania accused of violence on Maasai protesting eviction
  • Head of US Marines vows to bolster defense in southern Japan
  • Rwanda says Congolese soldier crossed border and opened fire
  • Europe wilts under early heat wave from Med to North Sea
  • Saudi crown prince to meet Turkey's president in Ankara
  • Building collapse kills 6 in Egyptian capital of Cairo
  • White men charged with smashing Black teen’s car with rock, traffic cone
  • Pride events targeted in surge of anti-LGBTQ threats, violence
  • These kids don’t have fathers. He takes them fishing every weekend.
  • Tears for the racist torment my father endured
  • Video shows inmates intervene after guard was choked with a pillowcase
  • A county approved Pride Month. In June, officials overturned the vote.
  • Children served floor sealant instead of milk at Alaska day care
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says recession ‘not inevitable,’ acknowledges Americans are ‘really down’
  • Biden’s ‘energy whisperer’ balances climate agenda, rising gas prices
  • Congressional covid funding deal appears ‘dead’ after GOP criticism
  • Trump aides told him that using Pence to overturn election was illegal
  • Justice Dept. says Jan. 6 House panel hampering major Proud Boys trial
  • Jan. 6 hearing: Pro-Trump lawyer sought pardon after pushing plan to overturn election
  • Anti-vaccine doctor sentenced to prison for Jan. 6 trespassing
  • SpaceX fires workers who criticized Elon Musk in open letter
  • Vince McMahon steps aside as WWE CEO amid probe of hush-money allegations
  • Top Workplaces 2022
  • How D.C.-area employers kept workers happy amid the Great Resignation
  • Four Top Workplaces chiefs offer lessons on crisis management
  • A constant state of flux: Settling into being unsettled at work
  • The fight to destigmatize mental health care in the defense industry
  • 7 ways to lower your credit card debt after the Fed rate hike
  • Here’s how the 2022 Top Workplaces were selected
  • Stop Slacking me: How to set digital boundaries for chatty co-workers
  • This Styrofoam-eating ‘superworm’ could help solve the garbage crisis
  • Google’s AI passed a famous test — and showed how the test is broken
  • Severna Park, Md., waterfront estate on the market for $15.9 million
  • Don’t Take Lunch From America’s Students
  • Juneteenth, a Day for Celebration and Frustration
  • The Plot Darkens in the Latest Episode of Jan. 6 Hearings
  • Ferrari’s Prancing Horse Will Need Its Skillful Rider
  • What Strikes and ‘Quits’ Say About US Labor’s Resolve
  • How a Battery Metals Squeeze Puts EV Future at Risk
  • What It Would Take for Jack Ma’s Ant to Reboot an IPO
  • Waive Pharma’s Vaccine Rights? What That Would Mean
  • We warned Google that people might believe AI was sentient. Now it’s happening.
  • Jan. 6 was Mike Pence’s proudest day. He should own it.
  • If Powell’s Fed tenure is a success, what would failure look like?
  • Will Trump run for president again? 12 of our columnists have the answer.
  • How the gun talks expose the myth of an obstructionist Senate
  • It’s all over but the shouting
  • Baseball looks strange, but the weird thing is how easily it happened
  • The gun-violence plague is evolving, dangerously
  • Historically Black colleges should no longer have to do more with less
  • A young soldier’s death reminds Ukrainians that the war is taking their best
  • Don’t let the door hit you on the way out, NRA
  • Taking a class trip to D.C. doesn’t come with a license to behave badly
  • We must reimagine how to address D.C.’s persistent health inequities
  • Maryland’s offshore wind project is a $4 billion boondoggle
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • What are the biggest revelations from the Jan. 6 investigation? Ask Jennifer Rubin.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Spurs bolster squad with Bissouma signing from Brighton
  • Bayern agree deal for Liverpool's Mane - reports
  • US approves Pfizer, Moderna COVID-19 vaccines for children under five
  • China's Sinomine invests $200 million in Zimbabwe lithium project
  • China finds signs of water in moon's 'Ocean of Storms'

  • Marcao double takes Three Towns top after Wuhan derby win
  • Crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital considers asset sales, bailout - WSJ

  • Germans turn to food banks as inflation hits
  • US supports Philippines in South China Sea, State Department says
  • Ukraine to introduce visas for Russians: Zelenskyy
  • England break record for highest ODI total with 498 against Netherlands
  • Japan protests to China over resource development in East China Sea

  • Babel Finance suspends withdrawals as crypto markets slump
  • Japan's top court says government not responsible for Fukushima damage
  • Sri Lanka schools, government offices to shut as transport grinds to halt
  • COVID-19 in Hong Kong shows 'signs of rebound', city leader Carrie Lam says
  • Indonesia’s dairy exporters say products are safe for consumption amid foot and mouth disease outbreak

  • China launches third aircraft carrier, named after province opposite Taiwan
  • Thailand to ease entry rules for travellers, scrap outdoor mask mandate as it moves to endemic COVID-19
  • Chinese city offers home purchase vouchers to residents displaced by urban renewal: Sources
  • China to ban new heavy industrial projects in key zones
  • Indian trains set ablaze in protests against military hiring changes
  • Malaysia police set up surveillance, barbed wire near Causeway after people seen entering JB illegally
  • CNA Explains: The Malaysian government wants to end the mandatory death penalty. Where does it go from here?

  • A Chinese invasion of Taiwan: Too costly to countenance?

  • Asian markets hit by recession fears, yen drops after BOJ decision

  • Creditors back Garuda Indonesia's $9 billion debt restructuring - administrators
  • SpaceX fires at least five for letter criticizing Musk-sources
  • Global equity fund outflows rise on fears of inflationary risks
  • Exclusive-China's central bank accepts Ant's application for financial holding company- sources
  • White House kicks off push to train more broadband, construction and EV workers
  • Hong Kong's March-May unemployment eases to 5.1%, outlook improving
  • Exclusive: China firms in advanced talks with Qatar for gas field stakes, LNG offtake - sources

  • China likely to keep lending benchmark LPR unchanged amid global central bank tightening
  • HDB changes criteria for putting SERS flats on resale market; minimum occupation period of 5 years required
  • Philippines sees wider c/a deficits as global risks build
  • The Cathay Cineplex in Handy Road to close on Jun 26, independent cinema The Projector to enter as pop-up
  • Serial offender jailed for pushing taxi drivers after refusing to pay his fare
  • Cannabis among S$55,000 worth of drugs seized, 146 suspects arrested: CNB
  • Police investigating case of mischief by fire allegedly involving International French School students
  • SingPost releases new set of Singapore mascot stamps
  • Healing the Divide's Iris Koh seeks court permission to leave Singapore for cancer treatment
  • CNA938 documentary on cryptocurrency scams wins SOPA award
  • Job vacancies at record high as labour market rebounds: MOM
  • Police appeal for information on 15-year-old boy missing for 5 days
  • Singapore’s exports grow at faster pace of 12.4% in May
  • Worker dies after wall he was hacking at Serangoon Gardens home collapses on him
  • Commentary: Call Sports Hub public-private partnership a failure or not, that is not the point
  • High Court dismisses bid by activist Han Hui Hui and 5 others to declare COVID-19 vaccination measures as 'unlawful'
  • Leipzig sign Austria midfielder Schlager from Wolfsburg
  • Nadal intends to play Wimbledon as foot injury improves
  • Porto agree to sell midfielder Vieira to Arsenal
  • Bahrain Victorious withdraw from Tour de Suisse after positive COVID tests
  • Former resort staffer McDermott returns to old workplace a Wallaby
  • Australia's Starc can't put finger on ODI return
  • Warriors beat Celtics 103-90 to win 4th NBA title in 8 years
  • Chelsea owner Boehly wary of FFP impact on transfer business
  • Gymnastics-British athletes subjected to 'child abuse', says former gymnast Pavier
  • Pumas enforcer Matera on crusade for first championship
  • Morgan to manage workload with World Cups on the horizon
  • Olympic sprint champion Jacobs cleared to resume training
  • Capital snub: Washington, D.C., passed over for 2026 World Cup hosting gig
  • 2026 World Cup 'invasion' will develop North American love for the sport-Infantino
  • Egypt sack manager Galal after three games
  • Five on Friday: 5 tech relics that welcomed Internet Explorer to the void
  • Australian power crisis eases as coal-fired plants crank up
  • EU backs Ukraine's membership bid as war brings huge shift
  • Humanitarian situation in eastern Ukraine 'extremely alarming': UN
  • Bordeaux region bans outdoor events as heatwave hits France

  • WTO strikes global trade deals deep into overtime

entry.title: Guardian: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England set new record score of 498 in first ODI against Netherlands – live!
  • US Open golf 2022: second round – live!
  • Boris Johnson misses Tory ‘red wall’ conference to make surprise visit to Kyiv – UK politics live
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Putin calls sanctions ‘reckless and insane’; EU candidacy ‘brings victory closer’, Zelenskiy says – live
  • US authorizes first Covid vaccines for infants and preschoolers
  • Biden hints that Russia is his priority: ‘If we let Putin roll, he wouldn’t stop’ – live
  • Britain has approved Assange’s extradition – war criminals and murderers, rejoice | Peter Oborne
  • ‘Unacceptable’: Russian warship accused of violating Danish waters
  • Sadio Mané leaving Liverpool after £35m Bayern Munich offer accepted
  • UK asked to host Eurovision in 2023 after Ukraine ruled out
  • Brodi Snook: Handful review – caustic comedy of depressing dates
  • Should I still work from home if I’m sick? We ask an expert
  • Ravneet Gill’s recipe for Italian summer pudding | The sweet spot
  • Dear Keir, people say that after Johnson a bit of boring would be nice. Unfortunately, people lie | Marina Hyde
  • ‘Can objects teach us about reality?’: Ruth Ozeki on her Women’s prize-winning novel
  • ‘Marching towards starvation’: UN warns of hell on earth if Ukraine war goes on
  • World Cup 2026: Host city reveal lays bare sprawling, money-spinning affair
  • Scientists harness light therapy to target and kill cancer cells in world first
  • ‘Democratic and a great way to flirt’: wave hello to the handheld fan
  • The new Golden State Warriors: relentless, ruthless … and oddly endearing
  • In his own words: Dom Phillips’ reporting on Brazil and the Amazon
  • As urban temperatures climb, here’s what the modern city should look like | Bob Ward
  • Fossil fuel firms ‘have humanity by the throat’, says UN head in blistering attack
  • I fear my grandson is being emotionally and physically damaged by father’s negative behaviour | Ask Annalisa Barbieri
  • Floral dresses: wear ’em like you don’t really mean ’em | Jess Cartner-Morley
  • The January 6 hearings aren’t acknowledging the elephant in the room | Thomas Zimmer
  • Did ‘good’ Republicans save us from the ‘bad’ ones on January 6? I don’t buy it | Moira Donegan
  • Dürer in Birmingham, Hockney in Normandy and a not-so-hot climate crisis show – the week in art
  • SpaceX employees fired after writing letter criticizing Elon Musk
  • EU says Ukraine should be given candidate status to join bloc
  • Danny Care set to play for England for first time since 2018 against Barbarians
  • ‘I fell on my arse on a Gucci catwalk’: Michelle de Swarte, model, standup and now star of The Baby
  • Julian Assange’s extradition from UK to US approved by home secretary
  • Third American volunteer fighter reported missing in Ukraine
  • ‘Frankly insulting’: Rwanda resents its portrayal in UK asylum row
  • Ita Buttrose recalls ‘open sexism’ at News Corp in 1980s where women were seen as ‘the enemy’
  • Borthwick’s Leicester stand in way of Saracens’ sweetest Premiership title | Ugo Monye
  • The Guide #39: How Barry became the most compelling show on TV
  • ‘We worry about it disappearing’: alarm grows over Italy’s drought-hit Po River
  • This is going to hurt: how to have awkward conversations
  • China launches third aircraft carrier in military advance
  • Taipei cools tempers by cutting traffic light wait time for summer
  • Sonia Sotomayor says supreme court’s ‘mistakes’ can be corrected over time
  • Experience: I am the dullest man in Britain
  • Are foot-peeling ‘masks’ worth the time – and the ickiness?
  • Is The Lord of the Rings now a never-ending franchise like Marvel and Star Wars?
  • Death of disabled passenger at Gatwick airport prompts investigation
  • ‘The nation had lost its mind’: the extraordinary new documentary about the death of Princess Diana
  • You be the judge: should my wife stop walking off when we argue?
  • Mollusc mass mortality event: can Spain’s giant fan mussels recover?
  • ‘It cannot be activism as usual’: Kumi Naidoo and Luisa Neubauer on the way forward for climate justice
  • Profiteering bosses, not workers, are pushing up inflation. Here’s how to fight back | Aditya Chakrabortty
  • For hydrogen power to be a climate solution, leaks must be curbed
  • Closing Syria aid route would be ‘catastrophe’, UN warned
  • ‘I got robbed of my 20s’: Sky Ferreira on fighting her record label – and refusing to compromise
  • Jay-Z’s bitcoin school met with skepticism in his former housing project: ‘I don’t have money to be losing’
  • Stock markets plunge again as flurry of interest rate hikes fuels recession fears
  • Japanese children allowed to talk again over lunch as Covid cases fall
  • Trump brought US ‘dangerously close to catastrophe’, January 6 panel says
  • A voice to the silenced: harrowing gymnast testimonies in Whyte review
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: NY Times: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: Financial Times: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Global stocks in steep weekly retreat after central bank rate rises

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 17 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-17 14:33PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong health experts propose 5 key groups, including animal care workers, be vaccinated against monkeypox before exposure
  • Climate change: Hong Kong government must step up efforts to develop EV charging infrastructure to meet 2050 carbon-neutral goal, panel hears
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum previews opening shows featuring national treasures loaned by Beijing
  • Candidates running for Hong Kong arts body will need to get nominations from powerful Election Committee to ensure ‘national security elements’ addressed
  • Climate Change: Hong Kong well placed to act as go-between in carbon credits market as long as it adopts international standards, experts say
  • Hong Kong health expert jailed for 31 months over scheme to deceive WHO, other bodies into paying more than HK$4.25 million of grants, fees
  • Hong Kong protests: provision deeming zip ties ‘fit for unlawful purposes’ can be interpreted broadly, prosecutor argues in top court
  • Unlocking Students Full Potential
  • Coronavirus: surge in cases prompts Hong Kong to bolster testing; infections top 1,000 for 3 days straight
  • Hong Kong consumption vouchers: what you need to know about second batch of e-coupons, who is eligible and special offers available
  • How Hong Kong’s ‘Covid-19 heroes’ rose to the challenges they faced
  • Soaring interest rates: Hong Kong’s new mortgage borrowers and highly leveraged buyers are most at risk in the property market
  • Hong Kong’s biggest health crisis in 25 years: why did city stumble over Covid-19? Experts say it was too slow to change tactics, inflexible, put politics first
  • US pressures Iran by targeting Hong Kong and UAE firms
  • Top Chinese influencer Xu Guohao fined US$16 million for tax evasion amid crackdown on live-streaming industry
  • China, US gas trade increase could help lift relations from ‘worst in history’, ex-official says
  • NetEase’s new game Diablo Immortal banned from new posts on Weibo just a week before hotly-anticipated release
  • Chinese mRNA booster fights Omicron 4 times better than inactivated Covid-19 vaccines, researchers say
  • China hit by floods, heatwaves and a tornado, with more weather misery on the way
  • China must ‘optimise’ zero-Covid policy or firms will be ‘unwilling to invest’, says ex-finance minister
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping to host virtual BRICS summit
  • Tangshan restaurant attack suspect was wanted by Chinese authorities over previous crimes
  • On Taiwan’s frontline islands ‘life goes on’ as jets scream overhead
  • Chinese PLA attack on Taiwan: at hand or too costly to consider?
  • US senators’ Taiwan bill to ‘restructure’ relations with Taipei
  • Explosion at pesticide firm in northwest China leaves at least 8 hurt and 6 missing, cause still unknown
  • China launches Fujian, PLA Navy’s 3rd aircraft carrier
  • New railway completes 2,700km loop of Taklamakan Desert in move to integrate Xinjiang with rest of China
  • China appoints international affairs expert as new consul general in Brisbane
  • China’s beauty slimness obsession highlighted after woman’s weight drops to 25kg in a year with multiple organ failure and refuses treatment
  • China’s youth league to tackle graduate job pressure with advice, aid and a chance to ‘toughen’ up
  • White House adviser calls relationship with India ‘most important for the United States’
  • Why is China’s inflation rate low compared to the US, Europe and Britain?
  • Nicholas Burns, US ambassador to China, stresses need to find common ground amid tense relations
  • US security adviser says hard line on Russia is needed to dissuade China from similar moves
  • More than 90 per cent of oil slicks threatening global marine life caused by humans, US and Chinese scientists find

  • Philippine militants accused of beheading tourists surrender to authorities
  • Malaysian lawyers’ ‘walk for judicial independence’ gets off on the wrong foot
  • BTS has spoken out against anti-Asian racism in the West, but what about discrimination in South Korea?
  • Pacific can deal with its own security issues, China ‘offers economic opportunities’ – Samoa PM
  • Japan’s tougher cyberbullying laws prompted after Hana Kimura’s suicide sparks free speech debate
  • China-Australia relations: Canberra’s top diplomat Penny Wong visits Solomon Islands to address Beijing security concerns
  • Malaysia’s economic challenge shows how politics has become a bottleneck for growth
  • Thai influencers charged with royal insult over Lazada adverts
  • How J.Y. Pillay helped Lee Kuan Yew turn Singapore Airlines into a global giant
  • South Korea and Japan look to move forward and ‘normalise’ information-sharing by keeping their tense history in the past
  • Indian trains set ablaze in protests against military hiring changes
  • Why the US’ Indo-Pacific Economic Framework is really all about India
  • WTO reaches initial deal as India’s defiance tempered on vaccines, fishing, food security
  • Russia’s Putin dismisses ‘stupid’ Western sanctions, ‘blitzkrieg’
  • Tesla’s Elon Musk sued by investor in ‘toxic’ workplace discrimination accusation
  • EU backs Ukraine’s membership bid as war brings huge historic shift
  • ‘Dark day for democracy’ as UK approves US extradition of WikiLeaks’ founder Julian Assange
  • Ukrainians threaten to destroy Russian bridge to Crimea ‘target number one’
  • Why Ukraine’s request to join is big test for EU
  • Vladimir Putin’s health: pivotal yet shrouded in uncertainty after reports of treatments
  • Russian spy caught seeking war crimes court internship, Netherlands says
  • WTO strikes landmark deals package after marathon talks, prompting cheers
  • Remembering Vincent Chin, decades after racist killing that was a flash point for Asian-Americans
  • Zelensky hologram references Star Wars: ‘We will defeat the empire too’
  • Omicron less likely to cause long Covid, UK study finds
  • Donald Trump unleashed mob after VP Mike Pence rejected election plot, Capitol riot panel hears
  • Amsterdam’s Schiphol Airport to cut flights over busy summer
  • Can polar bears survive climate change? Scientists see hopeful signs with discovery of new group
  • China’s property crisis: Debt-laden Guangzhou R&F Properties seeks extension on 10 offshore bonds worth US$5.16 billion
  • Tesla raises Model Y prices again amid strong demand, Long Range SUVs to cost 5 per cent more
  • JD.com considers food delivery business, a market almost completely controlled by Meituan and Alibaba’s Ele.me
  • St Petersburg bourse to add Alibaba, Tencent, other Hong Kong stocks from June 20 in trial to widen access for Russian investors
  • Tech stocks lift Hong Kong market while Asian equities languish as investors fret about Fed policy outlook, recession
  • Chinese stocks emerge as safe haven from bear markets globally, as US rate rise eclipses Shanghai lockdown as bogeyman
  • Recession and inflation fears give way to a crisis of confidence in economic policymaking
  • HSBC teams up with Emma Raducanu to create new opportunities for young people around the world
  • HSBC leads clients to see beyond the norm with its art initiatives
  • Flamenco fashion icon inspires Dior cruise show in Spain with embroidery that uses threads of actual silver and gold
  • K-drama Yumi’s Cells season two: love is in the air as workplace romcom returns with Kim Go-eun and Jinyoung
  • How we can tap non-human intelligence – that of other animals, and plants – to save the world
  • Cheap flights with Kiwi.com saved me US$1,500 but was it worth it? My 41-hour journey from Asia to Europe
  • Cleansing foot pads do not remove toxins from your body, experts say – the foot is not a detoxifying organ
  • She gave away her US$3,000 dream wedding dress on Facebook – now other women are donating their dresses to brides in need
  • How Asia bar industry expert Holly Graham found her calling and why she’s now telling the stories of its people
  • Yoga, meditation with Lululemon at Hong Kong’s M+ museum in summer programme focused on wellness, sustainability and healing
  • Netflix returns to Ghost in the Shell with new Japanese anime series
  • Chinese-American father-son cooks on YouTube channels Made with Lau and Hunger Pangs on how they’ve bonded over food
  • Video: Wushu world champion from Hong Kong explains how Chinese kung fu has broadened his horizons
  • Prince William and Kate Middleton’s new ‘normal family home’: Adelaide Cottage is located on royal grounds near Queen Elizabeth and the schools of Princes George and Louis, and Princess Charlotte
  • Hong Kong’s top Father’s Day meal deals, from The Peninsula, W Hong Kong and Agora – spoil dad rotten with Singaporean, Spanish, Cantonese and Japanese flavours
  • 12 best Italian restaurants in Hong Kong, from three-Michelin-starred 8 1/2 Otto e Mezzo Bombana and Ritz-Carlton’s Tosca di Angelo, to Sabatini Ristorante Italiano at the Royal Garden Hotel
  • Meet Lee Jae-wook, star of Netflix K-drama Alchemy of Souls: the 24-year-old rising actor was signed by Hyun Bin’s agency and looks up to Joaquin Phoenix’s The Joker
  • Where is Hong Kong 80s beauty queen May Ng Yuen-fong today? The Miss Hong Kong runner-up married a wealthy business tycoon, lives in a mansion – and hasn’t seemed to age a day
  • Is the real British monarch in Australia? Here’s why some believe Edward IV’s reign was ‘illegitimate’, making farmer Michael Hastings and son Simon the ‘rightful heirs’ instead of Queen Elizabeth
  • Who is Elizabeth Hurley’s son and mirror image, Damian Hurley? The 20-year-old model’s godfathers include Hugh Grant and Elton John, and his dad is late millionaire and property heir, Steve Bing
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 17 Jun, 2022

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
